items.audioText: A big dog ran to the man.
items.stem: Who did the dog run to?
items.rationale: The dog ran to the man.

items.audioText: Tom can hop on the log.

items.passage: The hen ran up to the pen and had a nap.
items.audioText: The hen ran up to the pen and had a nap.
items.stem: What did the hen do at the pen?
items.rationale: The hen had a nap at the pen.

items.passage: Meg got a big bun and a hot dog.
items.audioText: Meg got a big bun and a hot dog.

choices.label: a bun and a hot dog

items.rationale: Meg got a bun and a hot dog.

items.audioText: The kid put the pot in the bin.
items.stem: What did the kid put in the bin?

items.passage: Ben ran to get his big red bus.
items.audioText: Ben ran to get his big red bus.

items.passage: The bug is on a big wet leg.
items.audioText: The bug is on a big wet leg.

items.stem: What did Nan have to eat?
items.rationale: Nan had a jam bun.

items.passage: The man in the van ran to fix the tap.

items.audioText: Kim let the dog run and dig in the wet mud.

items.rationale: The hen sat on ten eggs.

items.passage: A wet dog ran up to Dad.

items.rationale: Peg has ten pens.

items.audioText: The cub sat in the den.

RD-cvc-sentences.iCanStatement: I can read a short sentence and say what it means.
RD-cvc-sentences.explanation: Now we put the words together into sentences. Read each word from left to right, then think about what the whole sentence tells you. Tap the picture-word answer that matches what you read. If a word is tricky, tap the speaker to hear the sentence, then read it again yourself.

workedExamples.text: The cat sat on the mat.
workedExamples.note: Read word by word — the cat sat ON the mat. So the cat is on the mat.

workedExamples.text: A big dog ran to the man.
workedExamples.note: Who did the dog run to? It ran to the man.
